brief_summary: Horizontal bar comparison, three rows, each showing an actuation-to-bottom-out spring spread on a shared 50g-70g scale. Row one (Gazzew Boba U4T, coral) runs 62g to 68g, a short 6-gram bar labelled the heaviest actuation with the narrowest climb. Row two (Drop Holy Panda X) runs 60g to 67g, a 7-gram bar. Row three (Durock T1) runs 55g to 67g, the longest bar at 12 grams, labelled lightest actuation with the widest climb to bottom-out.
data_sources: gazzew-boba-u4t-deep-dive.mdx § 'Where the U4T sits against the field' — 'the heaviest actuation weight in the set at 62g, paired with the narrowest spread to bottom-out at just 6 grams' and 'a lighter 55g actuation that climbs a full 12 grams by the time the stroke bottoms out'
